title: SOLID, Code Smells, and Evidence-Driven Refactoring · Code Recall
summary: Evolve a growing circulation system without changing its verified external behavior. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a refactored maintainable service after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign responsibilities by cohesive reason to change rather than forcing one method or one field per class.
Create extension points around observed stable variation without prebuilding speculative plugin systems.
Expose small client-specific roles so consumers do not depend on operations they cannot or should not use.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-oop cumulative stakeholder program."""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

# Add one tested, typed, inspectable program increment per activity.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: python solid refactoring. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Oop Safe Refactoring trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for refactor through characterization tests, small transformations, reviewable diffs, and unchanged external behavior.

content.text: For Oop Safe Refactoring, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Oop Safe Refactoring precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A refactor may change behavior as long as the architecture becomes cleaner.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Oop Safe Refactoring applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Refactor through characterization tests, small transformations, reviewable diffs, and unchanged external behavior.
